title: Isolate the trigonometric function
text: Begin by isolating the sine function on one side of the equation.

title: Solve for $$\sin(3\theta)$$
text: What is the value of $$\sin(3\theta)$$ after isolating it?

title: Find general solutions for the angle
text: Let u $$=$$ 3$$\theta$$. Find all general solutions for u where $$\sin(u)$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2}$$. $$Remember$$ $$to$$ $$include$$ $$the$$ $$periodicity.$$

title: Solve for $$\theta$$ and apply interval
text: Substitute u $$=$$ 3$$\theta$$ back into your general solutions and solve for $$\theta$$. $$Then$$, $$find$$ $$all$$ $$solutions$$ $$that$$ $$fall$$ $$within$$ $$the$$ $$interval$$ $$0$$ $$\leq$$ $$\theta$$ < $$2\pi$$.

title: Determine number of solutions
text: Since the period of $$\sin(3\theta)$$ is $$\frac{2\pi}{3}$$, $$how$$ $$many$$ $$full$$ $$cycles$$ $$of$$ 3$$\theta$$ are covered in the interval $$(0$$, $$2\pi)$$ for $$\theta$$? $$This$$ $$will$$ $$tell$$ $$you$$ $$how$$ $$many$$ $$solutions$$ $$to$$ $$expect$$.
